required_skills: Advanced physics domain expertise in one or more specialisations (condensed matter, quantum, plasma, photonics, nuclear, or particle physics)
Experimental apparatus design, construction, and operation including vacuum systems, optical setups, or cryogenic equipment
Quantum mechanics, electrodynamics, statistical mechanics, and thermodynamics applied to research problems
Precision measurement techniques including lock-in detection, signal averaging, and noise floor characterisation
Data acquisition system design and instrument control programming using Python, LabVIEW, or MATLAB
Statistical data analysis and error propagation for experimental physics measurements
Physics simulation and modelling using analytical and numerical methods
Peer-reviewed scientific publication and technical report writing for physics research outputs
Laboratory safety protocols including laser safety (IEC 60825), radiation safety, and cryogenic handling
Calibration and metrology of precision scientific instruments including spectrometers, oscilloscopes, and lock-in amplifiers
preferred_skills: Experience with quantum computing hardware platforms (superconducting qubits, trapped ions, photonic chips)
Knowledge of machine learning applications for physics discovery (e.g., physics-informed neural networks, neural ODEs)
Familiarity with cleanroom nanofabrication techniques for device physics or photonics research
Experience with synchrotron radiation sources, neutron scattering, or particle accelerator experimental programmes
Knowledge of photonics and nonlinear optics including laser design, optical parametric amplification, and frequency combs
Familiarity with materials characterisation techniques (XRD, TEM, SEM, XPS, AFM)
Experience with field-programmable gate array (FPGA) control systems for real-time physics experiment feedback
responsibilities: Design, construct, and operate experimental physics apparatus including ultra-high vacuum (UHV) systems, cryostats, optical tables, and precision measurement setups for original research programmes
Develop theoretical physics models and analytical frameworks to interpret experimental observations, connecting macroscopic measurements to underlying microscopic physical mechanisms
Implement data acquisition and instrument control systems using Python or LabVIEW, automating experimental sequences and enabling high-throughput parameter sweeps across measurement conditions
Perform rigorous statistical analysis of experimental datasets, applying error propagation, goodness-of-fit testing, and uncertainty quantification to extract physically meaningful results
Execute numerical simulations of physical systems using COMSOL, GEANT4, or custom solvers, validating computational predictions against experimental measurements
Author and publish original research findings in peer-reviewed physics journals (Physical Review Letters, Nature Physics, Science), managing the full manuscript preparation and peer review process
Present research at international physics conferences (APS March Meeting, SPIE Photonics West, DPG Spring Meeting) and represent the organisation in scientific collaboration networks
Identify novel physics phenomena or measurement opportunities by conducting systematic literature reviews and collaborating with theorists to develop experimentally testable predictions
Develop novel experimental techniques, diagnostic methods, and instrumentation to extend measurement capabilities beyond the current state of the art in the relevant physics subdomain
Collaborate with engineers, material scientists, and computational colleagues in multidisciplinary research teams, contributing physics expertise to translation and application development projects
